page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, providing 16 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 8.0 GB of memory per core. For storage, the server is equipped with a bundled 3839 GB local SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps. The system operates on a KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% category, and single-threaded gzip compression registering in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores, which all rank in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also show average performance, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 11.7 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 7.76 GB/sec, fall within the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.i2ne.4xlarge offers a high density of bundled local SSD storage and a substantial memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, making it a viable option for workloads requiring significant local storage capacity without the premium of external storage attachments. However, the tradeoff lies in its lower single-core processing capabilities. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, whereas it is less optimal for single-threaded processing or intensive large-model LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 3839 GB local SSD, operating under a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark results show a contrast between weak single-core performance (bottom 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench) and average multi-core capabilities (middle 50% in stress-ng and Passmark CPU). Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET operations, perform at average levels, while Passmark memory scores are weak. Qualitatively, the server provides high storage and memory density relative to its compute resources. It is suited for database operations, multi-threaded applications, and static web serving, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded processing efficiency.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 3839 GB local SSD, delivering average multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An x86_64 server featuring dedicated cores, a high-capacity bundled local SSD, and balanced multi-core performance for database workloads.
